message:
fix(database): MySQL PING fail-closed state tracking and Redis transaction error recovery

MySQL (src/database/mysql.rs):
- Set closed=true before sending COM_PING so that if the future is
  dropped mid-protocol-exchange the connection stays fail-closed
- Only reset closed=false on successful OK response or server-level
  errors (where the protocol is still synchronized)
- Prevents desynchronized connections from being returned to the pool
  after cancelled PING operations

Redis (src/messaging/redis.rs):
- Fix Transaction::cmd() error handling: on Redis-level errors (QUEUED
  response that is actually an error string), preserve the connection
  and transaction context instead of losing them
- On successful EXEC with array result, properly return connection to
  pool via return_to_pool()
- Ensures transaction state is not leaked on recoverable errors while
  still discarding on protocol-level failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
